name: signedjson
documentation:
# Signed JSON

```{image} https://img.shields.io/pypi/v/signedjson.svg
:alt: Latest Version
:target: https://pypi.python.org/pypi/signedjson/
```

```{image} https://img.shields.io/travis/matrix-org/python-signedjson.svg
:target: https://travis-ci.org/matrix-org/python-signedjson
```

Signs JSON objects with ED25519 signatures.

## Features

- More than one entity can sign the same object.
- Each entity can sign the object with more than one key making it easier to
  rotate keys
- ED25519 can be replaced with a different algorithm.
- Unprotected data can be added to the object under the `"unsigned"` key.

## Installing

```bash
pip install signedjson
```

## Using

```python
from signedjson.key import generate_signing_key, get_verify_key
from signedjson.sign import (
    sign_json, verify_signed_json, SignatureVerifyException
)

signing_key = generate_signing_key('zxcvb')
signed_json = sign_json({'my_key': 'my_data'}, 'Alice', signing_key)

verify_key = get_verify_key(signing_key)

try:
    verify_signed_json(signed_json, 'Alice', verify_key)
    print 'Signature is valid'
except SignatureVerifyException:
    print 'Signature is invalid'
```

## Format

```json
{
    "<protected_name>": "<protected_value>",
    "signatures": {
        "<entity_name>": {
            "ed25519:<key_id>": "<unpadded_base64_signature>"
        }
    },
    "unsigned": {
        "<unprotected_name>": "<unprotected_value>",
    }
}
```

functions.name: signedjson.key.generate_signing_key
documentation:
Generate a new signing key
Args:
    version: Identifies this key out the keys for this entity.
Returns:
    A SigningKey object.

functions.name: signedjson.key.get_verify_key
documentation: Get a verify key from a signing key

functions.name: signedjson.key.decode_signing_key_base64
documentation:
Decode a base64 encoded signing key
Args:
    algorithm: The algorithm the key is for (currently "ed25519").
    version: Identifies this key out of the keys for this entity.
    key_base64: Base64 encoded bytes of the key.
Returns:
    A SigningKey object.

functions.name: signedjson.key.encode_signing_key_base64
documentation:
Encode a signing key as base64
Args:
    key: A signing key to encode.
Returns:
    base64 encoded string.

functions.name: signedjson.key.encode_verify_key_base64
documentation:
Encode a verify key as base64
Args:
    key: A signing key to encode.
Returns:
    base64 encoded string.

functions.name: signedjson.key.is_signing_algorithm_supported
documentation: Is the signing algorithm for this key_id supported

functions.name: signedjson.key.decode_verify_key_base64
documentation:
Decode a base64 encoded verify key
Args:
    algorithm (str): The algorithm the key is for (currently "ed25519").
    version (str): Identifies this key out of the keys for this entity.
    key_base64 (str): Base64 encoded bytes of the key.
Returns:
    A VerifyKey object.

functions.name: signedjson.key.decode_verify_key_bytes
documentation:
Decode a raw verify key
Args:
    key_id: Identifies this key out of the keys for this entity.
    key_bytes: Raw bytes of the key.
Returns:
    A VerifyKey object.

functions.name: signedjson.key.read_signing_keys
documentation:
Reads a list of keys from a stream
Args:
    stream : A stream to iterate for keys.
Returns:
    list of SigningKey objects.

functions.name: signedjson.key.read_old_signing_keys
documentation:
Reads a list of old keys from a stream
Args:
    stream : A stream to iterate for keys.
Returns:
    list of VerifyKey objects.

functions.name: signedjson.key.write_signing_keys
documentation:
Writes a list of keys to a stream.
Args:
    stream: Stream to write keys to.
    keys: List of SigningKey objects.

functions.name: signedjson.sign.sign_json
documentation:
Sign the JSON object. Stores the signature in json_object["signatures"].

Args:
    json_object (dict): The JSON object to sign.
    signature_name (str): The name of the signing entity.
    signing_key (syutil.crypto.SigningKey): The key to sign the JSON with.

Returns:
    The modified, signed JSON object.

functions.name: signedjson.sign.signature_ids
documentation:
Does the JSON object have a signature for the given name?
Args:
    json_object (dict): The JSON object to check.
    signature_name (str): The name of the signing entity to check for
    supported_algorithms (list of str): List of supported signature
        algorithms
Returns:
    list of key identifier strings.

functions.name: signedjson.sign.verify_signed_json
documentation:
Check a signature on a signed JSON object.

Args:
    json_object: The signed JSON object to check.
    signature_name: The name of the signature to check.
    verify_key: The key to verify the signature.

Raises:
    SignatureVerifyException: If the signature isn't valid

classes.name: signedjson.sign.SignatureVerifyException
documentation: A signature could not be verified
